body.title: 3-pass architecture audit — Gemini 1M breadth → Opus deep-dive → Opus synthesis with case-proof verdicts
body.result: Pass 1 (Gemini breadth) + Pass 2 (Opus depth) complete. Output in lab/design_docs/audit_2026/{pass1_output,pass2_output}/. Disposal/review work continues under T-epic-fix-all-pass2-findings (GH#507, ~154 findings) + T-audit-design-thread-pass2 (GH#508, joint design thread) + T-audit-findings-to-tickets (auto-file tooling, parked). Pass 3 (human technical 'how it all works' docs + case-proof verdicts) deferred — file separately when we're ready.
body.description:
Run a three-pass audit of the whole project, each pass with a different cognitive profile. Validates both the code and the thesis — 'is Igor actually proving his biomimetic case?' — and as a bonus, exercises the new comms-channel architecture by using it as the Pass-1 transport.

## Blockers — ALL CLEARED 2026-04-20a
- T-uc-inference-channel — DONE
- T-uc-chattable-llm-channel — DONE
- Gemini 2.5 Pro: routable via OpenRouter (no separate API credential needed — CC is OR-funded)
- T-audit-prompts-human-reviewed — DONE (pass1/pass2 prompts reviewed + edited by Akien, 811718cf)
- Pass 3 prompt: intentionally NOT pre-written — Pass 3 shape depends on what passes 1-2 turn up

Ready to kick off. Pass 1 trigger: open comms://model/gemini-pro, send repo payload, read report.

## Pass 1 — Gemini 2.5 Pro (breadth, 1M context)
- Input: full repo (wild_igor/ + lab/ + tests/) as one payload
- Role: multi-persona auditor — senior engineer, biomimicry engineer, QA skeptic, architect, documentation reviewer. No 'what to pay attention to' constraints.
- Biomimicry lens specifically: flag things NAMED biologically but IMPLEMENTED procedurally (e.g. a habit called 'Hebbian' that actually just increments a counter). This is the recurring failure mode and the highest-value finding class.
- Transport: comms://model/gemini-pro via the new inference-channel
- Output: a structured findings report — concerns grouped by severity and area

## Pass 2 — Opus 4.7 (depth)
- Input: Pass 1 findings + repo access
- Role: deep-dive engineer — for each Pass 1 finding, read the code, verify or refute, propose the fix, assess blast radius.
- Additional remit: find what Gemini missed. Different model = different blind spots.
- Output: list of ticket candidates with preliminary design for each (not full implementations).

## Pass 3 — Opus 4.7 (synthesis + case-proof verdicts)
- Input: Pass 2 ticket candidates + repo + Igor's live-state dashboards
- Role: architect + evaluator
- Two deliverables:
  1. 'How it all works' — an honest architecture doc written post-hoc from the code, not from the design docs. What IS, not what was intended.
  2. 'Is it proving its case?' — verdicts on each major claim of the Igor thesis, with evidence:
     - Local-inference share of calls over time: trending up, flat, or down?
     - TWM salience competition: genuinely competitive, or argmax-in-disguise?
     - Habits forming from experience, or only from seeds?
     - Graph showing real Hebbian patterns (organic co-activation clusters)?
     - Is Igor shipping his own code? (Currently: no — gated. The gate status itself is a data point.)
     - Each verdict is pass / partial / fail with pointer to evidence.

## Disposal strategy (mandatory — prevents queue flood)
Pass 3 MUST categorize all Pass 2 ticket candidates:
- SHIP — actionable now, add to queue
- DEFER — valid but not now, record as reference memory in graph
- INVESTIGATE — unknown if real, file as spike ticket
- DISCARD — false positive, noted and dropped
Target: 5–10 SHIP tickets max. If Pass 2 produces 80 findings, 70 go into DEFER/DISCARD with reasoning.

## Why this ticket is XL and bounded anyway
- Each pass is one (long) run, not weeks of coding. Total elapsed: maybe 2–3 sessions once blockers clear.
- The 'case-proof verdicts' are the highest-value artifact the project has ever produced — they either validate the whole thesis or redirect it. Worth every token.
- Rerunnable: once the comms channel path is built, re-auditing is as cheap as 'open channel to gemini-auditor, send repo, read report' — we get this as a recurring capability, not a one-off.

## Out of scope
- Actually implementing the SHIP tickets (each becomes its own sprint)
- Fixing anything DURING the audit passes (tempting; don't — contaminates findings)
- Gemini-tuning / prompt engineering beyond what Pass 1 needs to cover the personas

## Related
- T-uc-inference-channel, T-uc-chattable-llm-channel — prerequisites
- T-case-study-lessons-learned — parallel: outside-view writeup of CC+Akien working together; this ticket is inside-view of the codebase
- T-audit-2026-03-25 (done) — prior single-pass audit, useful as a reference shape but far narrower
